sections.paragraph:
συντριβή, ῆς, ἡ (Vett. Val. 74, 4; Heliod. 10, 28; Sb 5763, 42; LXX) lit. ‘rubbing away’ crushing, destruction, of Christ, who is put in place like a firm stone εἰς συντριβήν to destroy those who dash against (=take offense at) him B 6:2 (‘polishing’ JKleist, transl. ’48, p. 172 n. 59).—Sv.

Heliod Heliod , III? a.d.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

transl. transl. = translate, translation

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 976
